item.title: The Latest on Allbirds’ Abrupt Pivot to AI: a New Name and CEO
item.description: Allbirds Inc., the maker of once-viral wool sneakers that suddenly announced a pivot to artificial intelligence earlier this year, has officially renamed itself Smartbird Inc. and is tapping the former leader of a Danish AI infrastructure group as its new chief.

item.title: Australia Probes Private Credit Lenders, Warns on Valuations
item.description: Australia’s corporate watchdog is investigating a number of private credit funds and warning industry managers to make sure that asset valuations are “grounded in realistic assumptions” before an end-of-month reporting deadline.

item.title: US Pending Home Sales See Biggest Gain Since September 2024
item.description: Pending sales of US existing homes had their strongest gain in nearly two years in May, sustaining a slight rally in the beleaguered home resale market.

item.title: ‘Summer of the Bond Market’ Is Teed Up for Warsh, Goncalves Says
item.description: As new Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh leads his first policy meeting, falling oil prices are set to smooth the way for a rally in fixed income, according to MUFG’s chief of US macro strategy, George Goncalves.

item.title: Botswana Eyes Power Exports With $100-Million Solar Plant
item.description: Botswana is set to begin construction of its first large-scale solar plant after developers secured $100 million in financing, as investors bet that rising electricity demand across southern Africa will create a lucrative market for surplus power.

item.title: Wrong-Way AI Trade Costs Florida Stock-Picker $50 Billion
item.description: It was 180 degrees off the hot trade on Wall Street.

item.title: India’s BSE to Unveil New Index to Boost Ethical Investing
item.description: BSE Index Services Pvt. has introduced a measure of Indian stocks that are aligned with so-called “saatvik” principles in an effort to offer investors a choice based on ethical and cultural practices.

item.title: Google’s Gemini-Powered AI Home Speaker Goes on Sale June 25
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google said that its Google Home Speaker, a $100 device that includes deep integration with the company’s Gemini artificial intelligence platform, will be available starting June 25.

item.title: World Cup Travel Is Up for All US Host Cities Except for One
item.description: Trump’s bickering with Canada and geographic isolation are working against Seattle

item.title: Haaland’s World Cup Debut Draws Sponsors Betting on US Stardom
item.description: Last week, Erling Haaland sat with fans and watched the NHL’s Carolina Hurricanes close in on the Stanley Cup. One of the world’s best football players is unlikely to get away with such anonymity for much longer.

item.title: Warsh Has Potential to Spur Market Volatility, Fidelity Says
item.description: Investors from Fidelity say new Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh stands to stoke bond-market volatility by offering his views on inflation.

item.title: The Trump Phone Is More Novelty Than Serious Smartphone Contender
item.description: The Trump phone is finally here — sort of — and it bears little resemblance to the original pitch.

item.title: US Retail Sales Rise in Broad Gain Despite Higher Gas Prices
item.description: US consumers ramped up spending in May across a broad range of retailers, signaling households powered ahead despite higher gasoline prices.

item.title: ECB’s Lagarde Warns AI Is Huge Risk for Financial Stability
item.description: Artificial intelligence can potentially lead to dangerous financial crises, and the European Central Bank is determined to ensure that doesn’t happen, President Christine Lagarde said.

item.title: Macron Seeks Way Around Trump’s Ban on Anthropic’s AI Models
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron will lead talks with Group of Seven leaders and tech executives to explore how to deploy the most advanced artificial intelligence models through so-called trusted partners, after the US blocked foreign nationals from accessing the technology.

item.title: Egypt’s Pound Becomes World’s Top Currency as Oil Shock Reverses
item.description: Egypt’s pound has become the world’s best-performing currency after oil prices dropped sharply on a US-Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a move expected to restore supply flows and ease inflation concerns.

item.title: JPMorgan’s David Kelly Says AI Boom Will Refuel Risk Rally
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s asset-management arm is urging investors to stick with the risk rally in the second half of 2026, arguing that an AI investment boom and resilient consumers should keep the expansion intact despite persistent inflation and a Federal Reserve on hold.

item.title: Hedges Removed Due to Iran Deal Leave S&P Vulnerable on Fed Day
item.description: If traders are getting anxious ahead of Kevin Warsh’s first interest-rate meeting at the helm of the Federal Reserve, they aren’t really showing it. That type of sanguine posture can make the market vulnerable to near-term declines.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for June 17, 2026
item.description: US equity-index futures are little changed ahead of the Federal Reserve’s interest-rate decision. Contracts on the S&P 500 Index are up 0.1% at 7:40 a.m. in New York.

item.title: Apple Investors Are Tired of AI Promises, Want Tangible Progress
item.description: Apple Inc. investors are losing patience with the company’s talk about becoming a more formidable presence in artificial intelligence and want to start seeing some results.

item.title: China Plans Mechanism to Evaluate AI Impacts on Job Market
item.description: China is planning a mechanism to assess the impact of artificial intelligence on the job market, highlighting Beijing’s growing concerns over disruptions to the livelihood of its citizens from the technology.

item.title: SpaceX Venture Backers Debate When to Cash Out
item.description: Some longtime investors say they’ll hold their ever-more valuable shares

item.title: Key Question for Kevin Warsh’s Fed Is Whether to Remove Its Policy Anchor
item.description: Investors will be listening for how strongly Warsh supports the Fed’s 2% inflation goal.

item.title: An Essential Reading List on the Short and Tumultuous History of Decentralized Finance
item.description: Cornell professor Eswar Prasad’s recommendations are anything but academic.

item.title: Uber to Offer Robotaxis in Houston With Lucid and Nuro, Posing Challenge to Waymo
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. will offer driverless robotaxi rides in Houston in vehicles developed by Lucid Group Inc. and Nuro Inc., setting it up to challenge Waymo in the fourth-largest US city.

item.title: DP World Looks to Return to US Port Operations After 20-Year Hiatus
item.description: The Dubai-based logistics giant is in talks to develop a container terminal in Texas

item.title: Blue Origin Says It Will Launch a New Glenn Rocket This Year
item.description: Blue Origin is already rebuilding the Florida launch site where its New Glenn rocket exploded last month, making way for the space company to fly again this year and rejuvenate its ambitions to challenge SpaceX.

item.title: Total Oil Trading Gain Doubled to About $1 Billion Last Quarter
item.description: TotalEnergies SE’s oil trading profits doubled to about $1 billion in the first quarter as the energy giant went on a crude buying spree in the run-up to the Iran war, Chief Executive Officer Patrick Pouyanné said.

item.title: G7 Aims to Ensure China Supplies No More Than 60% of Rare Earths
item.description: The Group of Seven countries have agreed that no single country should supply more than 60% of their imports of critical minerals by 2030 in a effort to reduce their reliance on China, according to people familiar with the discussions.

item.title: Poland Invests $11 Million in ElevenLabs to Build AI Tech Hub
item.description: Poland has acquired a stake in artificial intelligence voice company ElevenLabs, as the country hopes to become a major European AI hub.
